items.title: Ultimatums, diplomacy and a trip to Graceland as Trump eyes a deal with Iran
items.summary: America may be a nation at war, but the president's activities have been a mix of diplomacy and diversions - with the occasional swing toward the surreal.

items.title: 'Like the plane got cut in half': LaGuardia crash that killed two pilots now under investigation
items.summary: In the aftermath of the collision of Air Canada flight AC8646 with a fire truck in New York, US transportation chief Sean Duffy emphasized that seat belts helped save lives

items.title: Inside the alleged Russian operation to trigger anti-government protests in Angola
items.summary: A group of Russians and Angolans are going on trial in Angola after being accused of fomenting protests, which they deny.

items.title: Colombian military plane crash kills at least 66
items.summary: Colombia's defence minister says it was a "tragic accident" but the cause is not yet clear.

items.title: China dials back on fuel price hikes to 'reduce burden' on drivers
items.summary: It comes as countries across the region are taking various measures to weather the soaring cost of fuel.

items.title: Oil falls and shares rebound after Trump says talks have been held to end war
items.summary: Energy prices fall and stock markets rebound after the US president says "very good and productive" talks have been held.

items.title: Australia and EU agree sweeping trade deal in face of global uncertainty
items.summary: Australia and the EU sign sweeping trade and security deals after years of negotiations.

items.title: Jury orders Cosby to pay $19m to ex-waitress after finding he abused her in 1972
items.summary: Donna Motsinger alleged Cosby gave her wine and a pill that left her incapacitated after picking her up in a limousine in 1972.

items.title: Referendum defeat leaves Italy's Meloni looking more vulnerable
items.summary: Meloni vowed to press on after losing a vote on constitutional reform many turned into a referendum on her government.

items.title: Slovenia becomes first EU country to introduce fuel rationing
items.summary: Until further notice, motorists in Slovenia will be restricted to a maximum purchase of 50 litres of fuel per day.

items.title: US Senate confirms Markwayne Mullin as homeland security chief
items.summary: The US senator will now oversee the embattled department after a series of immigration enforcement blunders.

items.title: US bans new foreign-made consumer internet routers
items.summary: There are almost no major brands of internet routers that are manufactured in the US.

items.title: Blast that injured two was 'terrorist attack', Ukrainian authorities say
items.summary: The suspect is a 21-year-old local resident who claims he was blackmailed and forced to plant explosives.

items.title: ICE agents deploy to major US airports as security queues stretch for hours
items.summary: Hundreds of ICE agents have been deployed to 14 airports, in cities including New York, Atlanta and Houston.

items.title: OnlyFans owner Leonid Radvinsky dies at 43
items.summary: Leo Radvinsky became a billionaire after investing in the site, known for its pornographic content.

items.title: Nigerian police arrest 15 after viral videos of alleged sexual assault at festival
items.summary: The videos sparked widespread anger, leading to the hashtag #StopRapingWomen to trend on social media.

items.title: HK police can now demand phone passwords under new national security rules
items.summary: Those who do not comply can be jailed for up to a year and fined up to HK$100,000 ($12,700; £9,600).

items.title: UN warns Earth's climate being 'pushed beyond its limits' as El Niño looms
items.summary: The Earth's climate is further out of balance than at any time in recorded history, the UN's weather agency says.

items.title: Kenyan ex-foreign minister arrested and accused of staging his disappearance
items.summary: The reported disappearance of Raphael Tuju had led to claims he may have been abducted.

items.title: BTS agency shares drop after comeback show turnout falls short
items.summary: The concert was attended by an estimated 104,000 fans, much lower than the expected 260,000.

items.title: France's former Socialist Prime Minister Lionel Jospin dies at 88
items.summary: Jospin was head of government from 1997 to 2002 and is credited with bringing in the 35-hour working week.

items.title: Australia's ABC staff to go on strike for first time in 20 years
items.summary: The 24-hour long strike is expected to disrupt live television and radio broadcasts.

items.title: France's Socialists hold on to power in major cities in election boost for mainstream
items.summary: Paris and Marseille are held by the Socialists in local elections which saw the nationalist right win in Nice and certain towns.

items.title: Israeli settlers target Palestinian villages in occupied West Bank, attacking people and properties
items.summary: The violence began after 18-year-old settler Yehuda Sherman was killed after reportedly being hit by a vehicle driven by a Palestinian while on his quad bike.

items.title: National blackout hits Cuba for second time in a week
items.summary: Another total failure of its power grid underlines Cuba's hardship under a US-imposed fuel blockade.

items.title: Israel to expand ground and air attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon
items.summary: There are fears in Lebanon that Israel is preparing for a large-scale ground invasion.

items.title: Cuba's mothers-to-be prepare to give birth in a country plunged into darkness
items.summary: Two pregnant women tell the BBC's Will Grant of their hopes and fears as their nation is mired in crisis.

items.title: A committed pharmacist and a homesick blogger – the Iranian civilians killed in the war
items.summary: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

items.title: UK universities flock to India - but will they succeed?
items.summary: Nine UK universities are setting up campuses in India, but enrolment is expected to be modest initially.

items.title: US' and Iran's options for ending war narrow the longer it goes on
items.summary: Iran shows no sign of climbdown, while air power alone cannot achieve the US' main goal.

items.title: Could Iranian missiles reach London and Paris?
items.summary: Some experts say Iran may be able to launch missiles to hit London, but they would likely be shot down long before arriving and the risk is low.

items.title: US weight-loss drugmakers slash prices in fight to win customers
items.summary: Weight-loss drug prices are falling in the US - but can the example be repeated?

items.title: Germany has a shortage of workers - so it's turning to India for help
items.summary: The European nation, struggling to find skilled staff, is giving jobs to young people from India.

items.title: How the deadly LaGuardia Airport crash unfolded
items.summary: Video shows the aftermath of the collision between a passenger plane and a firefighting vehicle on the runway of the New York airport.

items.title: 'Stop, stop, stop': Listen to LaGuardia control tower audio during collision
items.summary: An Air Canada plane carrying 72 passengers and four crew initially landed safely from Montreal on Sunday evening before colliding with a firefighting vehicle.

items.title: Watch: Inside Palestinian homes attacked by settlers
items.summary: Violence began after a teenage settler was killed, reportedly after being hit by a vehicle driven by a Palestinian.

items.title: Why is Israel targeting Lebanon's bridges?
items.summary: Israeli forces have been instructed to destroy the crossings over the Litani river that were being used by Hezbollah to send reinforcements.

items.title: 'A direct hit' - BBC visits Israeli town after Iranian strike
items.summary: The BBC's Sebastian Usher reports from Dimona in southern Israel, which was hit by an Iranian missile.
